figure_types.name: condition_specific_family_divergence_heatmap
figure_types.primary_question: Which family pairs are separated by this graph/evidence condition?
figure_types.required_inputs: graph/evidence condition, family posterior vectors, pairwise JS or KL
figure_types.required_annotations: family order, target marker, metric label, evidence level

figure_types.name: target_belief_profile_panel
figure_types.primary_question: What behavioural predictions produce the divergence values?
figure_types.required_inputs: same condition as type A, target posterior per family
figure_types.required_annotations: graph, observed evidence, target node, family labels

figure_types.name: global_family_confusion_summary
figure_types.primary_question: Which families are globally confusable across the current panel?
figure_types.required_inputs: panel-level divergences or synthetic recovery matrix
figure_types.required_annotations: same family order, panel description, metric summary

figure_types.name: parameter_grid_separability_map
figure_types.primary_question: Where are free parameters identifiable or degenerate?
figure_types.required_inputs: parameter grid, panel-level divergences or recovery
figure_types.required_annotations: parameter axes, anchor setting, color scale
figure_types.default_metric: mean JS to anchor / local identifiability

figure_types.name: rooted_descriptor_linkage_summary
figure_types.primary_question: Which rooted dimensions drive separability or identifiability?
figure_types.required_inputs: descriptor values and condition-level diagnostic scores
figure_types.default_metric: mean JS or diagnostic score by descriptor bin

figure_types.name: ranked_diagnostic_condition_summary
figure_types.primary_question: Which conditions are most useful to carry forward?
figure_types.required_annotations: pair-specific target, graph id, evidence level, score

conventions.value: M_exact, M_0, M_rho, M_rho,beta, M_star, M_cluster
JS divergence by default; use directional KL only when asymmetry is itself the scientific point.
Use local-neighbour identifiability or mean JS to a named anchor.
Keep identical colour scales across comparable heatmaps inside one analysis.
analysis-N_figX_description.pdf with figure number fixed by report order.
Every condition-specific panel must show graph, evidence, target and family order.

continuity_note: D1/D2/D3 labels must remain aligned with analysis-3 rooted descriptor terminology.
